title: Development of a whole genome sequencing method for Hepatitis E Virus
author: Hakze - van der Honing, R. W.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Hepatitis E virus (HEV) genotype 3 is a zoonotic virus. HEV has a relatively high genetic diversity and its classification can best be done by whole genome sequencing. So far, to obtain the virus complete sequence a high concentration of HEV is needed. 

 Our aim is to develop a sample processing protocol for recovery of enough RNA from HEV positive target samples of different origin and concentrations.
steps_raw:
Hepatitis E virus (HEV) genotype 3 is a zoonotic virus. HEV has a relatively high genetic diversity and its classification can best be done by whole genome sequencing. So far, to obtain the virus complete sequence a high concentration of HEV is needed. 

 Our aim is to develop a sample processing protocol for recovery of enough RNA from HEV positive target samples of different origin and concentrations.
